title: Coordinates, Graphs, Functions, and Rates of Change · Decision Notebook: Slope Rate
summary: Model water use, travel, cost, and capacity from tables, graphs, formulas, and descriptions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg slope rate increment in a multi-representation resource dashboard. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret slope as a signed rate with output units per input unit.
Translate a relationship among words, tables, graphs, and formulas while preserving meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Slope Rate evidence
steps.instruction: A model passes through (2, 8) and (6, 20). Calculate its average rate of change.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (20 − 8) / (6 − 2).

content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.
